objectives: Deep architectural review of mx20022 workspace (7 crates)
Evaluate dependency graph, separation of concerns, error design, codegen pipeline, validation layering, feature flags, extensibility
important_facts: Dependency graph is acyclic and well-layered: model -> parse -> validate/translate -> cli
codegen is fully standalone (no model dependency)
model crate has zero non-serde dependencies -- excellent for a data type crate
Two validation systems exist: Validatable trait (model-level, codegen'd) and RuleRegistry (validate-level, runtime)
typed.rs in validate bridges the two systems via validate_constraints/validate_message
Translation helpers in helpers.rs are hardcoded to pacs_008_001_13 types
SchemeValidator dual API: raw XML + Any downcasting
Pattern codegen compiles XSD regex to inline byte checks, avoiding regex dep in model
FedNow validator has been improved: integer-cent arithmetic instead of f64 comparison
validate crate depends on model WITHOUT features, meaning only default (pacs) is enabled
parse and translate crates depend on model with features=[all]
Feature flags are on model only, not propagated through parse/validate/translate
risks_issues: MEDIUM: validate depends on model without features=[all], typed validation of non-pacs types may fail to compile in downstream if not feature-gated
MEDIUM: Translation helpers hardcoded to pacs.008 types -- code duplication across pacs.009/camt.053 mappings
LOW: SchemeValidator::validate_typed uses Any downcasting -- loses type safety at the trait boundary
LOW: No unified error enum across workspace -- each crate defines its own, composed via #[from]
LOW: ConstraintError in model does not use thiserror (manual Display/Error impl) while all other crates use thiserror

iteration.summary: Completed comprehensive architectural review of all 7 crates. Read all Cargo.toml files, lib.rs entry points, codegen pipeline (xsd, ir, emit), model common types, parse/validate/translate internals, CLI structure. The architecture is well-designed with clean crate boundaries and a proper acyclic dependency graph. The codegen three-phase pipeline is solid. Identified medium-severity issues around feature flag propagation and translation helper type coupling. The dual validation system is justified by different use cases (codegen-time typed vs runtime extensible). Overall architectural quality is high for a v0.1 library.
inputs_consulted: Codegen: ir/types.rs, ir/lower.rs, emit/mod.rs, emit/validate.rs, emit/pattern_codegen.rs
Model: common/mod.rs, common/validate.rs, common/builder.rs, common/choice.rs, generated/mod.rs
Validate: lib.rs, error.rs, rules/mod.rs, schemes/mod.rs, schemes/fednow.rs, typed.rs

mentions.relevance_note: Security findings may overlap with validation layer concerns
Code quality findings complement architectural review
